hypotheses.name: Community Detection at L11 → Biological Annotation
hypotheses.method: Greedy modularity community detection (networkx) on k=10 kNN graph of 195 in-vocab genes at L11. Map communities to curated gene families (AP1, HLA-I/II, BCL2fam, TNFSF, IL2_pathway, RUNX, KLF) and cell-type markers (T-cell, B-cell, NK, monocyte) using Fisher's exact test (one-sided). Null: 100 random community labelings of same sizes.
hypotheses.primary_metric: modularity z-score vs null; Fisher p-value for enrichment
hypotheses.result_value: modularity=0.4342, z=34.12 vs null (0.434 vs -0.001±0.013); 2 communities (sizes 107, 88); B-cell markers: 3/3 in comm 1, OR=inf, p=0.090; IL2_pathway: 3/3 in comm 0, OR=inf, p=0.163; T-cell: 4/5 in comm 0, OR=3.38, p=0.251
hypotheses.next_action: The 2-community split is geometrically very robust (z=34). Biological enrichments are marginal (p 0.09-0.25). Test what DOES drive the 2-community split: compute differential expression of PC1 between communities, or check if community membership predicts known functional annotations (cytokines vs receptors, secreted vs membrane). Also test with k=5/20 to verify robustness.

hypotheses.name: Dorothea Regulatory Pairs → Embedding Distance Decay Across Layers (195 in-vocab)
hypotheses.method: Load Dorothea high-confidence (A/B, n=205) and low-confidence (C/D/E, n=693) TF-target pairs filtered to 195 in-vocab genes. Compare L2 embedding distance to 5000 random gene pairs. AUROC per layer (TF-target closer than random). Spearman rho(layer, AUROC).
hypotheses.result_value: High-conf AUROC: L0=0.564, L1=0.566, L2=0.571, ..., L11=0.505; rho=-0.853 (p=0.0004). Low-conf: AUROC~0.50 throughout, rho=0.175 (p=0.587). Regulatory geometry is early-layer signal.
hypotheses.next_action: KEY FINDING: regulatory proximity decays with layer depth on OOV-corrected gene set (rho=-0.85 vs iter_0024 full-gene result which showed stable AUROC ~0.67 across layers). This dissociation — early layers preserve regulatory geometry, late layers fragment it — aligns with PR collapse in iter_0031. Validate on cross-validation splits. Also test whether activation vs repression pairs have different decay profiles.

hypotheses.name: PC1 at L11 Separates TFs from B-cell Markers (Biological PC1 Polarity)
hypotheses.method: Project 195 in-vocab genes onto PC1 at L0, L5, L11. Mann-Whitney AUROC for TFs (n=13) vs non-TF (n=182), T-cell markers (n=5), B-cell markers (n=3), monocyte markers (n=0). Two-sided test; report AUROC and p-value. Compare L0 vs L11 as early/late control.
hypotheses.primary_metric: B-cell AUROC (inverse, i.e. B-cells at negative PC1 pole) at L5
hypotheses.next_action: B-cell markers consistently on negative PC1 pole (AUROC<0.5 at all layers, significant at L5). TFs trend toward positive PC1 pole (p=0.062 at L11). Only 3 B-cell markers in vocab limits power. Expand: get all genes in B-cell programs (IGHV, CD27, MZB1, JCHAIN etc.) from the full vocab (not just 195 named genes). Also test PC2 for complementary separation.
